description_long: This is a two-story pizzeria building with a cream-colored facade accented by bold red trim and base. The front features a large brick section with a prominent pizza slice sign, indicating its function as a pizzeria, and a red-and-white striped awning above the main entrance. The ground floor has two large windows with matching striped awnings and two green potted plants flanking the entrance. The upper floor has four windows, two on each side, and the roof is flat with a brick chimney, likely for a pizza oven. The side walls are plain except for the windows and awnings, and the overall design is clean and inviting, with a touch of classic Italian pizzeria style. The building appears to be constructed primarily from masonry and concrete, with decorative brickwork and metal or plastic awning supports.
description: A two-story cream and red pizzeria with a brick pizza sign, striped awnings, and a flat roof with a chimney. The entrance is flanked by potted plants and large windows.
description_view_0: The rear view shows a plain cream wall with red trim and a visible brick chimney on the roof.
description_view_1: The left side view displays two upper windows, two lower windows with striped awnings, and a potted plant near the entrance.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the main entrance with a large pizza sign, striped awning, two potted plants, and four windows.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with two upper windows, two lower windows with awnings, and a potted plant.
